revision.comment: alternative
Most use alternate, as this var, and [[Wikipedia:Disambiguation]]
wording
as per [[Template talk:Alternateuses|talk]]
"Alternative" means different. "Alternate" means switching between one and the other

see talk; 1) alternate does mean "Serving or used in place of another; substitute: an alternate plan" and 2) alternative has an unfortunate connotation of "Existing outside tradition or establishment"
compromise
Template:Otheruses moved to Fgdgdf
Cleanup.
Consistencize.
rv (to avoid use of <hr>)
test -- see if anyone likes this
revision.text: '''For other uses, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].'''
'''For other uses, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].'''

revision.comment: make it italic not bold (less visual impact)
revision.text: ''For other uses, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: rv

revision.text: :''For other people, places, or things sharing this name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: reverting to more general, less specific note

revision.text: <div class="dablink"> :''For other subjects sharing this name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)].'' </<di>
<div class="dablink"> :''For other articles sharing this name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)].'' </<di>

revision.comment: amend again
revision.text: :''For other articles on subjects with the same name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: 'subjects' isn't general, best to leave it out :)
revision.text: <div class="dablink"> :''For other articles sharing this name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)].'' </<di>

revision.comment: articles can never be the same name :)
revision.text: :''For other articles related to subjects that share a similar name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: cut the blah blah blah

revision.text: <div class="dablink"> :''For other articles with this name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)].'' </<di>

revision.comment: discuss, don't just revert
text/fmt moved to [[Template:Otheruses1]]

revision.text: {{otheruses1|{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)}}

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/202.40.210.174|202.40.210.174]] to last version by Docu
eliminate dependance on {{otheruse1}}. maintaining two templates isn't hard.
typo
For other subjects sharing this page's name,
revision.text: :''For other subjects sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other subjects sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other subjects sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other subjects sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other subjects sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: can we make this less glaring?
revision.text: :''For other subjects sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other articles sharing this page's name, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: :''For other uses of {{PAGENAME}}, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
revision.text: :''For other uses of {{PAGENAME}}, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other articles sharing this title, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: back to the [[User:Docu|Docu]]'s version
rvt to Cantus' last version
revision.text: :''For other articles sharing this title, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: Restore consistency with non-template messages.
rv to my last version - other articles don't "share the same title" as they will be disambiguated in some way. What is wrong with "For other uses of the term ..."?
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: : ''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}" or pages which include it in their title, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
revision.text: ''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}" or pages which include it in their title, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: rm unnecessary verbosity
revision.text: ''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}" see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: rv to the last stable version (without the double line odd formatting)

revision.text: : ''For other uses, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: simpler
revision.text: :''For other articles named like this, see '''[[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].'''''
:''For other articles named like this, see '''[[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].'''''
:''For other articles named like this, see '''[[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].'''''

revision.comment: rv
smaller please
Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Dori|Dori]] to last version by Jiang
div > span
remove fullstop
Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Cantus|Cantus]] to last version by Netoholic
revert edits by Michael Hardy to last edit by Cantus // This is not part of the article and it should not end on a full stop
Punctuation
Undent.
its been indented since inception and that is standard for dab notes
I think a line below the "otheruses" would clarify the other uses better
no, the horizontal rule is evil, it must be destroyed
yuk Cantus, that is ugly
I'm sorry, but this the standard way to deal with this. Look at Britannica, Encarta and dozen others. This is the way it is dealt with. I don't like it either, but it is the right way :(
nonsense; we don't need to allow other encyclopedias to dictate style standards
Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/71.109.99.53|71.109.99.53]] to last version by Bkonrad
right, so we know better than Britannica and Encarta about style... yeah...ok
Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Cantus|Cantus]] to last version by Rdsmith4
more applicable grammar, more formal formatting
revision.text: ''For other uses of the term'' "{{PAGENAME}}" ''or pages which include it in their title, see'' '''[[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]]'''.

revision.comment: rv to opriginal. Please don't change long-established templates without discussing at talk pages
rvt on merits, don't rvt for a perceived lack of communication.
revision.text: ''For other uses of the term'' "{{PAGENAME}}" ''or pages which include it in their title, see'' '''[[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]]'''.

revision.comment: the new text is technically wrong, see explanation at talk page.
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: Grammatify.
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: back to less ugly version
back to correct version
Deuglify.
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}," see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}," see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}," see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''
:''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}," see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: per [[WP:MOS]]
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: per [[WP:MOS]], comma always goes inside the quotation mark
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}," see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: rv (as per [[Template talk:Otheruses#Arthur_said_the_situation_was_"deplorable".]])
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Docu|Docu]] to last version by Cantus
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}," see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: [[Category:Article names with other uses]]
revision.text: :''For other uses, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].'' [[Category:Article names with other uses]]

revision.comment: delete [[Category:Article names with other uses]] by concensus in CFD
rv; there are a heck of a lot of articles to fix with this change, so don't redirect it until they're all done
MAJOR: s/uses/articles named like this/
revision.text: :''For other articles named like this, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: rv, that sounds awkward
Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/195.3.97.222|195.3.97.222]] to last version by 24.224.153.40
Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/217.6.164.96|217.6.164.96]] to last version by Antandrus
See the talk page for the reasons for this edit, which makes this execrable page somewhat less obnoxious. No one has raised a voice in objection after some time. I will comment further on the talk p
revision.text: :''For other senses of this term, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contributions/Michael Hardy|Michael Hardy]] ([[User talk:Michael Hardy|talk]]) to last version by Everyking
class="notice dablink" (the text, which is what the fuss is about, is unchanged)
"for other uses" alone is not clear enough to readers unfamiliar with Wikipedia. "For other uses of this term" is much clearer
revision.text: ''For other uses of this term, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: remove notice class, it adds unnecessary padding.
revision.text: ''For other uses of this term, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: ...and restore indent
revision.text: :''For other uses of this term, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: [[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates]]
revision.text: :''For other uses of this term, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Otheruses]]

revision.comment: use {{PAGENAMEE}}, so the link works if the page has '' in it
revision.text: :''For other uses of this term, see [[{{PAGENAMEE}} (disambiguation)]].''[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Otheruses]]

revision.comment: Don't use PAGENAMEE & replace ambiguous "this term". See talk.
revision.text: :''For other uses of "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Otheruses]]

revision.comment: [[User:RobertG]] want people to look at a disambiguation page to find out what other things they can use honey for!! This page is incredibly obnoxious. It should be deleted. Use. "dablink".
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Otheruses]]

revision.comment: rv back to phrasing that has been here for a long time, despite M Hardy's repeated attempts to insert his preferred language
revision.text: :''For other uses of "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Otheruses]]

revision.comment: Bkonrad is wrong to say this page said this for a long time. This is much stupider than what it said for several years. It said "for other uses, see". It didn't say "for other uses of personal lubric
revision.text: :''For other uses of the term "{{PAGENAME}}", see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Otheruses]]

revision.comment: restoring earlier version without "term" or redundant page name
+<noinclude>[[Category:Disambiguation and Redirection templates|Dablink]]</noinclude>

''For other uses, see [[{{PAGENAME}} (disambiguation)]].''

revision.comment: implementing template doc page pattern. (per talk request)
"uses" is ambiguous ;-) clarifying wording as per talk
«"There are" → "For", ";" → ","» changing as per talk. (wordy is good. newcomers aren't as familiar with our disambiguation structure as you are)
Protected Template:Otheruses: It has been edited several times by admins, so there must be some use, and there has been no vandalism [edit=autoconfirmed:move=autoconfirmed]
to match name of template
Try another wording.
revert, that wording has been suggested and rejected before.
For other uses...uses of what? "For other uses of helium, see this list of articles about a band and Haskell compiler"
other is unnecessary, the other articles are similar, not same (and anyway the dab page usually still lists the primary article)
revert, please make an effort to participate in the discussion on the talk page. We've been over this there.
Change "articles" to "topics"; this is a recurrent issue because "For other uses" is bad wording; this appears to be untried wording, anything wrong with it?
replace "other"
1. please make an effort to discuss on [[Wikipedia talk:Disambiguation]], the issue of other topics that don't have similar names still applies, and 2. everything can use otheruses4 now
mismatched brackets
rv rm-al of whitespace
rv dumb edit
rv - previous change removed the indentation
Protected Template:Otheruses: High-risk template [edit=sysop:move=sysop]
+{{hprotected}}
+[[ru:Шаблон:Другие значения]]
minor code edits
{{documentation}}
resolve redirect
moved [[Template:Otheruses]] to [[Template:Other uses]]: Separate words
link to Foo (disambiguation) instead of Foo, if it exists
Undid revision 362489209 by [[Special:Contributions/R'n'B|R'n'B]] ([[User talk:R'n'B|talk]]) this will probably changes tenths of existing targets causing problems, or am I missing something?
fix
temporary, part of cleanup for about, will remove later

Changed protection level of Template:Other uses: [[WP:High-risk templates|Highly visible template]]: Over 37000 tranclusions; allowing template editors ([Edit=Allow only template editors and admins] (indefinite) [Move=Allow only template editors and admin
Updated with Lua upgrade from sandbox
Rm stray newline
Forgot to provide an edit summary earlier: template has been nominated for merging
Updating TFD template: discussion was relisted ([[WP:XFDC|XFDcloser]])
TFD closed as no consensus ([[WP:XFDC|XFDcloser]])
upd
wrap invocation of the module in <includeonly></includeonly> to suppress categorization of [[Template:Other uses]] into [[:Category:Hatnote templates using unusual parameters]]
edited wrong template by accident — Undid revision 986783417 by [[Special:Contributions/Andrybak|Andrybak]] ([[User talk:Andrybak|talk]])
populate [[Category:Articles with Template:Other uses targeting a nonexistent page]]
use {{user other}} to exclude user pages, before I go crazy cleaning them up!
well, I did name it "Articles with..." – other namespaces aren't worth the time & trouble.
Removed categorization system as redundant
1 revision imported: impoting nglish ikipdia tmplats
Adding [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Undo revision [[Special:Diff/218826|218826]] by [[Special:Contributions/Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]])
1 revision imported: importing templates
179 revisions imported from [[:wikipedia:Template:Other_uses]]: importing because this actually has redlinks for a lot of the articles
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23524127410|commit triggered pipeline]]
Text replacement - "</noinclude>↵<noinclude>" to ""
